title: GPT Architecture and Language Modeling
summary: How GPT-style models work end to end: tokenization → embeddings → Transformer decoder → next token prediction.
key_concepts: Causal (masked) self-attention: tokens can't see the future
KV cache: avoid recomputing keys/values at inference
Scaling laws: loss ~ (compute, params, data) power laws
exercises: Implement a minimal GPT (nanoGPT style) in PyTorch from scratch.
What is KV cache and why does it matter for inference speed?
Explain the scaling laws (Chinchilla). How do you decide model size vs dataset size?
What is perplexity and how does it relate to cross-entropy loss?
code_example:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class GPTBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, d_model, n_heads, d_ff, dropout=0.1):
        super().__init__()
        self.attn = nn.MultiheadAttention(d_model, n_heads, dropout=dropout, batch_first=True)
        self.ff = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(d_model, d_ff), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(d_ff, d_model))
        self.ln1 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.ln2 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model)
        self.drop = nn.Dropout(dropout)

    def forward(self, x, causal_mask=None):
        # Pre-norm architecture (GPT-2 style)
        attn_out, _ = self.attn(self.ln1(x), self.ln1(x), self.ln1(x), attn_mask=causal_mask)
        x = x + self.drop(attn_out)
        x = x + self.drop(self.ff(self.ln2(x)))
        return x
thinking_questions: Why does causal masking matter — what would happen without it?
What is 'emergent behavior' in LLMs and at what scale does it appear?
